meta_title: For Founders: project management to Solve tutorial hell (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome tutorial hell. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Project Management to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop watching tutorials and start shipping. Learn how to use project management, Antigravity, and Claude Code to build your SaaS MVP effectively.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell is a byproduct of aimless learning. To escape, you must pivot from passive consumption to active project management. By defining a strict MVP scope, breaking features into atomic tasks, and utilizing AI-assisted coding tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you transform theoretical knowledge into profitable, production-ready software.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks watching YouTube tutorials, stacking up tabs of 'to-do' list apps and SaaS boilerplate repos. Yet, your own product remains a ghostly phantom in your IDE. You are trapped in the comfort of consumption, mistaking the act of watching for the art of building. The dopamine hit of a completed tutorial is a lie; it’s a simulation of progress that leaves your bank account empty and your vision unbuilt.

Real founders don't learn by watching; they learn by breaking. To ship, you must abandon the tutorial cycle and embrace rigorous project management. By treating your MVP as a series of high-stakes engineering sprints rather than a coding exercise, you force your brain to solve actual business problems. It’s time to stop studying and start shipping.

steps.title: Define the Atomic MVP
steps.description: Strip your SaaS idea down to a single, revenue-generating core feature. Ignore authentication, dark mode, and UI polish. Focus on the one thing a user will pay for.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Evaluate my idea for [Insert Idea] and break it down into the absolute minimum functional requirements needed for a paid MVP. Output a JSON list of 5 essential features.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a project roadmap file named MVP_SCOPE.md based on these 5 features'

steps.title: Structure the Project Architecture
steps.description: Establish a clean folder structure. Proper organization prevents 'spaghetti code' syndrome, which is the primary cause of abandonment in early-stage projects.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a scalable folder structure for a [Insert Tech Stack] SaaS. Focus on modularity and clear separation of concerns.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize the project directory and create the folder structure defined in the architecture plan'

steps.title: Automate the Backend Skeleton
steps.description: Use AI to scaffold the boilerplate. Don't waste time typing repetitive CRUD operations; delegate them to Claude Code so you can focus on business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust API boilerplate for [Insert Feature] using [Insert Language]. Include error handling and data validation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the database schema and API endpoints for the core user functionality'

steps.title: Iterative Feature Implementation
steps.description: Build one feature at a time. Never move to the next task until the current one is tested and integrated into the main build.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a test-driven implementation plan for [Insert Specific Feature]. Provide the code snippets for both the implementation and the unit test.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the feature and add unit tests to verify it works as expected'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Get your code into a production-like environment immediately. Feedback is the only metric that matters.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a checklist for a minimal viable deployment. What are the top 3 security and performance considerations before I go live?
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Audit the current codebase for security vulnerabilities and prepare a deployment script'

pro_tips: Never build a feature that doesn't have a direct path to a paying customer.
Use 'Time-boxed Sprints': If a feature takes more than 4 hours to build, it is too complex for an MVP—cut it.
Treat your IDE like a command center; if you aren't using CLI-based AI tools, you are losing 30% of your velocity.
Always build with testing in mind; a bug in production is a customer lost forever.
Avoid the 'Framework Trap': Use the tools you already know, not the 'hottest' ones on Hacker News.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, stop coding for 48 hours. Focus entirely on user acquisition and gathering feedback to iterate on your next sprint.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your ideas die in a folder of unfinished projects. The market doesn't care about your code quality—it cares about the problem you solve. Use these tools, stick to your scope, and get your product in front of real users today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
